$comment: PureCraft launch library — 30 recipes + 5 curated lists. Brand voice: Aesop + Apple + Pinterest + TikTok.

recipes.name: Luxury Hotel Linen Mist
recipes.premiumDescription: The mist boutique hotels spray on freshly turned beds. Lavender, white tea, and a whisper of cedar that lingers exactly the right amount.
steps: Pour witch hazel and glycerin into the spray bottle.
Add lavender, white tea, and cedarwood oils. Swirl 10 seconds.
Top off with distilled water, leaving a half-inch headspace.
Cap, shake, and rest in a cool spot for 1 hour before first use.
Mist sheets 12 inches above the bed before sleep.
safetyNotes: Spot-test on hidden fabric — oils may mark silk or velvet.
Avoid spraying directly on memory foam or pillowcases worn to bed.
Store in a cool, dark place; replace after 6 months.
recipes.storageTips: Cool, dark cabinet. UV breaks down essential oils.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops bergamot + 3 drops vetiver for a Le Labo–inspired hotel scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smells like the bed at the Edition. Costs less than a latte.
recipes.socialHook: POV: your sheets smell like a 5-star hotel for $4.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: sheets that smell like detergent. After: sheets that smell like a Sunday at the Aman.

recipes.name: Citrus Countertop Cleaner
recipes.premiumDescription: A bright morning-light spray that lifts grease and leaves the kitchen smelling like a Tuscan farmhouse breakfast.
steps: Pack peels into the jar, cover fully with vinegar, seal.
Infuse 7 days in a dark cabinet (or skip — quick version is still beautiful).
Strain the citrus vinegar into the spray bottle.
Add water, castile soap, and bergamot oil. Swirl gently.
Spray, rest 30 seconds, wipe with a soft microfiber.
safetyNotes: Do not use on natural stone (granite, marble) — vinegar etches.
Test sealed wood in a hidden spot first.
Never combine with bleach.
recipes.storageTips: Under-sink cool, away from direct sun.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Replace bergamot with 6 drops yuzu + 2 drops basil for a chef's-kitchen scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smells like Italy. Cleans like the chemical aisle.
recipes.socialHook: Bottling sunlight — citrus-vinegar that cuts grease in 30 seconds.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: sticky residue and synthetic lemon. After: clear stone, fresh-zested air.

recipes.name: Shower Glass Miracle Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Daily mist that erases soap scum and water spots before they form. The reason hotel showers always look new.
steps: Combine water, vinegar, and rubbing alcohol in the spray bottle.
Add castile soap, then drop in tea tree and lemon oils.
Cap and gently invert to mix — never shake hard with castile.
After your shower, mist all glass + tile while still warm.
Wipe top-down with the squeegee. No rinse needed.
safetyNotes: Flammable while wet (alcohol) — keep away from candles.
Not for natural stone showers.
Ventilate — vinegar fumes are sharp in small bathrooms.
recipes.storageTips: Inside the shower caddy works — use within 30 days for best results.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Swap rubbing alcohol for vodka and add 6 drops eucalyptus for a spa finish.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Daily use means you literally never scrub a shower again.
recipes.socialHook: The 30-second habit that means I'll never deep-clean my shower again.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: weekly hard-water scrub-fest. After: glass that stays gallery-clean.

recipes.name: 5-Minute Toilet Bomb Fizzies
recipes.premiumDescription: Drop-in fizz tablets that scrub, deodorize, and brighten while you brew coffee. Twelve to a jar — one for each week.
steps: Whisk baking soda, citric acid, and cornstarch in a dry bowl.
Drop in essential oils, then drizzle peroxide one bead at a time.
Mix to wet-sand consistency — should clump when squeezed.
Press firmly into mold. Let cure overnight.
Pop out, store in jar. Drop one in the bowl, walk away 5 min, brush, flush.
safetyNotes: Citric acid + peroxide will fizz — add slowly.
Keep finished tabs out of reach of pets and toddlers (looks like candy).
Avoid eyes — splashes from the bowl can irritate.
recipes.storageTips: Sealed glass jar with silica packet. Moisture is the enemy.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 1 tsp activated charcoal for a striking matte black tablet that deodorizes 2× longer.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: It looks like a wellness product. It works like bleach.
recipes.socialHook: Toilet bombs that fizz like bath bombs and clean like industrial.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: scrubbing on Sunday. After: drop, walk away, brush once.

recipes.name: Laundry Scent Booster Crystals
recipes.premiumDescription: A scoop in the wash and your towels smell like a Provence boutique for two weeks.
steps: Combine salts and baking soda in the bowl.
Drop oils in slowly while stirring with a fork to disperse evenly.
Fold in dried lavender buds.
Transfer to jar. Let scent develop 24 hours before first use.
Add 2 tbsp directly to the drum before clothes go in.
safetyNotes: Not for HE machines without dilution — 1 tbsp max.
Skip on baby clothing.
Wear gloves — salt can dry hands.
recipes.storageTips: Sealed jar in laundry room. Sunlight fades scent.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Layer in 4 drops vanilla absolute + 6 drops sandalwood for a Le Labo Rose 31 vibe.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Towels smell like a hotel for two weeks. Detergent doesn't do that.
recipes.socialHook: I made laundry crystals that smell like Loewe. My towels are a personality now.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: towels that smell clean. After: towels that smell like a destination.

recipes.name: Soft Skin Vanilla Sugar Scrub
recipes.premiumDescription: Whipped sugar, melted shea, and real vanilla — body scrub that melts into skin like dessert.
steps: Gently warm coconut oil to liquid (do not boil).
Combine sugars in a bowl, then pour in coconut + almond oil.
Stir in vanilla and vitamin E. Whip 1 minute for whipped texture.
Spoon into jar. Press flat.
In the shower: scoop, massage in circles 30 seconds, rinse warm.
safetyNotes: Tub will get slippery — rinse and wipe after use.
Patch-test first if nut-sensitive (almond oil).
Avoid broken or sunburned skin.
recipes.storageTips: Cool dry shelf. Use a wooden scoop, never wet fingers.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 1 tbsp Manuka honey + ¼ tsp Tahitian vanilla bean caviar.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smells like crème brûlée. Skin feels like silk for 12 hours.
recipes.socialHook: Made a body scrub that smells like the Laduree café. Cost: $5.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: rough winter skin. After: glossy, soft, smells like a French pastry.

recipes.name: Hair Growth Rosemary Oil
recipes.premiumDescription: The stimulating scalp oil with research-backed rosemary, layered with castor and jojoba for thicker, shinier hair.
steps: Pour jojoba, castor, and almond oils into the dropper bottle via funnel.
Add rosemary and peppermint oils.
Top with vitamin E. Cap and roll between palms 30 seconds.
Massage 4–6 drops into scalp twice a week, 10 minutes before shampoo.
Wash out gently with sulfate-free shampoo.
safetyNotes: Skip during pregnancy (rosemary contraindicated).
Do a 24-hour patch test — peppermint can tingle aggressively.
Castor oil stains pillowcases.
recipes.storageTips: Amber bottle, cool dark cabinet. Light degrades oils fast.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops cedarwood + 2 drops clary sage for the studied 'Beauty of Joseon hair tonic' blend.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Backed by a 2015 clinical trial. Cheaper than Mielle. Tingles like it's working.
recipes.socialHook: The rosemary oil that has Mielle on a 6-month waitlist — but I made mine.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: thinning edges. After: visibly thicker baby hairs in 8 weeks.

recipes.name: Under-Eye Cooling Gel
recipes.premiumDescription: Refrigerated aloe + green tea gel that takes the puff out of late nights in 60 seconds.
steps: Brew green tea, let cool fully. Juice cucumber by grating + pressing through cheesecloth.
Whisk aloe, tea, cucumber juice in a small bowl.
Add vitamin E + glycerin. Whisk until uniform.
Transfer to jar, refrigerate at least 2 hours before first use.
Tap two pearls under each eye morning + night with ring finger.
safetyNotes: Discard after 7 days — fresh ingredients spoil fast.
Avoid direct contact with eyes; use under-eye only.
Patch test on the inner elbow first.
recipes.storageTips: Refrigerated. The cold IS the product.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Stir in ½ tsp hyaluronic acid serum + 2 drops blue tansy oil for serious puff reduction.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cools like a $90 La Mer eye cream. Lasts a week. Costs $3.
recipes.socialHook: I keep eye gel in my fridge and people think I sleep 8 hours.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: monday-morning eye bags. After: I-am-rested undertones.

recipes.name: Stainless Steel Shine Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Mist + buff for fingerprint-free appliances that reflect sunlight like a showroom.
steps: Combine olive oil and vinegar in the spray bottle.
Add water, then lemon essential oil.
Cap and shake hard for 15 seconds — emulsion will be cloudy.
Spray a fine mist on the appliance, wipe in the direction of the grain.
Buff dry with a second clean microfiber for showroom finish.
safetyNotes: Always wipe with the grain — circles will leave streaks.
Shake before each use — oil separates.
Test in a hidden spot on coated finishes.
recipes.storageTips: Pantry. Refrigeration not needed.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Replace olive with mineral oil + 3 drops cedarwood for a longer-lasting matte sheen.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Two ingredients you have. Looks like Method but works better.
recipes.socialHook: My fridge looks like a Sub-Zero showroom and I made the spray for $2.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: fingerprint chaos. After: zero-prints, even from the toddler.

recipes.name: Shoe Odor Destroyer Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Mist before and after wear — kills odor at the source instead of masking with synthetic powder.
steps: Pour vodka into the spray bottle.
Add all essential oils. Swirl 10 seconds.
Top with distilled water, leaving a half-inch headspace.
Cap, shake well, label.
Mist insides of shoes before storing. Repeat post-workout.
safetyNotes: Flammable — keep from candles and stove.
Tea tree is toxic to cats and dogs — don't spray pet beds.
Air shoes dry before wearing.
recipes.storageTips: Cool, dark cabinet. Vodka extends shelf life.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 6 drops cypress + 4 drops cedarwood for a high-performance hiking-boot blend.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Actually kills odor, not just covers it. One bottle = a year.
recipes.socialHook: POV: my running shoes don't smell like a crime scene anymore.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: nose-from-five-feet-away. After: Le Labo for sneakers.

recipes.name: Mattress Refresh Mist
recipes.premiumDescription: Strip the bed, mist the mattress, walk away — wake up to a hotel-fresh bed without lifting a vacuum.
steps: Combine witch hazel and oils in the spray bottle. Swirl to disperse.
Top with distilled water; cap and shake gently.
Strip the bed completely.
Mist the mattress lightly, focusing on the head + foot quadrants.
Let dry 1 hour, vacuum the surface, remake bed.
safetyNotes: Don't oversaturate — heavy water on mattresses can grow mold.
Tea tree unsafe for pets — keep cats off the bed until fully dry.
Shake before each use.
recipes.storageTips: Bedside drawer. Use monthly with sheet changes.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops bergamot + 6 drops vetiver for a Westin Heavenly Bed scent dupe.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Your bed feels like a 5-star hotel. Takes 5 minutes a month.
recipes.socialHook: I refresh my mattress monthly and now my bed smells like the Four Seasons.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: dusty old mattress. After: hotel turn-down service energy.

recipes.name: Fridge Odor Eliminator Pods
recipes.premiumDescription: Tucked behind the milk, these pods absorb odors and quietly leave behind clean lemon air. One per shelf, one per month.
steps: Whisk baking soda, charcoal, and cornstarch in a bowl.
Drop in lemon oil, then drizzle water 1 tsp at a time.
Press firmly into mold — should hold shape like wet sand.
Cure uncovered for 24 hours.
Pop out, place 1 pod per shelf. Replace every 30 days.
safetyNotes: Charcoal stains — wear an apron.
Pods crumble if dropped; keep on a small saucer.
Replace immediately if exposed to spills.
recipes.storageTips: Saucer in fridge. Compost or sprinkle in trash bin when done.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 1 tsp matcha powder for a soft sage tint and a subtle green-tea note.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Replaces the open box of baking soda with something actually elegant.
recipes.socialHook: I made fridge pods that look like macarons and absorb every onion smell.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: leftover-curry-fridge. After: walk-in flower-shop fridge.

recipes.name: Natural Drain Refresh Mix
recipes.premiumDescription: The Sunday routine that keeps every drain in the home running clear and odor-free without chemicals.
steps: Premix baking soda + salt in a jar — keep on hand.
Drop ¼ cup of the dry mix into the drain.
Tuck lemon peel strips in next.
Pour warm vinegar slowly. Cap drain with a small bowl. Wait 10 minutes.
Chase with the kettle of boiling water.
safetyNotes: Wear oven mitts when pouring boiling water.
Skip if you've used commercial drain cleaner in the last 24 hours.
Not for septic systems weekly — once a month max.
recipes.storageTips: Jar of premix on the laundry shelf.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 6 drops peppermint + 4 drops lemon EO to the premix for a cooling spa scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: It actually fizzes. Drains run faster within 24 hours.
recipes.socialHook: Sunday drain reset that looks like a science experiment.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: slow drain + sour smell. After: glass-clear pipe + lemon air.

recipes.name: White Sneakers Brightener Paste
recipes.premiumDescription: Three-ingredient paste that takes scuffed Stan Smiths back to fresh-out-of-box white in 4 minutes.
steps: Mix baking soda + peroxide into a thick paste.
Add dish soap and water. Stir to a yogurt consistency.
Brush onto canvas/leather in circular motion.
Let sit in sunlight 20 minutes — UV activates the peroxide.
Wipe clean with damp cloth, air dry.
safetyNotes: Test colored panels first — peroxide is bleaching.
Wear gloves if you're sensitive — peroxide can dry skin.
Don't use on suede or nubuck.
recipes.storageTips: Make per use — peroxide degrades fast.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Finish with 1 drop tea tree oil rubbed into the leather + a beeswax buff.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Stan Smiths to new in 24 minutes. Better than Crep Protect.
recipes.socialHook: 60-cent shoe paste that beat my $30 sneaker cleaner.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: yellowed canvas, scuffed toes. After: factory-fresh, no joke.

recipes.name: Kitchen Grease Melter Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: The spray that erases stovetop splatter and range-hood film without scrubbing — chef-tested, kitchen-loved.
steps: Pour hot water into the spray bottle (not boiling).
Add castile soap. Cap and rotate gently.
Open cap, sprinkle baking soda, then add vinegar slowly — it'll fizz.
Add lemon oil, swirl, label.
Spray, wait 60 seconds, wipe with microfiber. Stubborn film: brush + repeat.
safetyNotes: Vent the kitchen — hot vinegar steam is sharp.
Avoid bare aluminum cookware finish.
Don't use on natural stone counters.
recipes.storageTips: Under the sink. Shake before each use.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 5 drops orange + 3 drops thyme oil for a chef-grade degreaser.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cuts a week of bacon-grease film in 60 seconds. Smells like fresh peel.
recipes.socialHook: The 60-second spray that made my range hood look new again.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: sticky stovetop you avoid. After: glass-clear, gallery-clean.

recipes.name: Pet-Safe Floor Cleaner
recipes.premiumDescription: Castile + chamomile-water mop solution that's safe for paws and noses — no oils that harm cats or dogs.
steps: Brew 4 chamomile tea bags in 1 cup hot water; let cool 20 min.
Fill bucket with warm water. Stir in castile soap.
Add chamomile tea + vinegar (omit on stone/marble).
Wring mop nearly dry — pets walk on it.
Mop in sections. Air dry — no rinse needed.
safetyNotes: Truly pet-safe only if you skip essential oils.
Wait until floors are fully dry for paws + crawling babies.
Vinegar damages unsealed wood, marble, travertine.
recipes.storageTips: Mix per use. Chamomile water keeps 2 days refrigerated.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Swap chamomile for cooled green tea + 1 tsp colloidal oatmeal for soothed paws after.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Truly pet-safe — most 'natural' cleaners aren't. Vet-approved tone.
recipes.socialHook: The pet-safe floor cleaner my vet actually approved.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: paw-ick + chemical guilt. After: clean floors, calm pup.

recipes.name: Baby-Safe Toy Surface Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Three ingredients only. Sanitizes toys, high chairs, and changing tables without anything you wouldn't put on baby's skin.
steps: Pour water into the spray bottle.
Add castile soap; cap and gently rotate.
Add vinegar slowly. Cap, swirl.
Spray onto cloth (never directly on plush toys), wipe surfaces.
Let air dry 5 minutes before baby plays.
safetyNotes: Skip essential oils — many are unsafe under age 2.
Replace bottle every 30 days for freshness.
Not a hospital-grade disinfectant.
recipes.storageTips: Diaper-bag friendly. Cool, dark drawer.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 1 tbsp colloidal silver for added antimicrobial action.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Three ingredients. Zero anxiety. Baby-safe and actually clean.
recipes.socialHook: The 3-ingredient spray every new mom asks me for.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: rotation of disinfectant guilt. After: peace of mind in a bottle.

recipes.name: Luxury Room Spray (Hotel Inspired)
recipes.premiumDescription: Layered hotel-lobby scent: bergamot top, jasmine heart, vetiver base. Three sprays = an entire room transformed.
steps: Pour vodka into the bottle — it carries scent.
Layer oils in order: bergamot, jasmine, vetiver, sandalwood.
Cap, swirl 30 seconds (do not shake).
Top with distilled water, cap, label.
Rest 48 hours for scent to bloom. 3 sprays per room.
safetyNotes: Flammable while wet (vodka).
Spot-test on fabrics — jasmine can mark light textiles.
Cat-toxic (vetiver, jasmine) — keep cats out of the room while spraying.
recipes.storageTips: Amber bottle on dresser. Light + heat fade scent.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Replace bergamot with neroli + add 4 drops oud accord for a Le Labo Santal 33 vibe.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smells like the lobby of the Soho House. Costs 4% of a Diptyque.
recipes.socialHook: I made the Diptyque Baies dupe. People literally ask for the perfumer's name.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: stale bedroom. After: hotel-lobby olfactive moment.

recipes.name: Anti-Stress Pillow Mist
recipes.premiumDescription: Studies-backed lavender + chamomile + valerian to coax sleep faster — three sprays on the pillow corner.
steps: Pour witch hazel + glycerin into the bottle.
Add lavender, chamomile, and valerian oils.
Top with distilled water; cap and swirl.
Rest 30 minutes before first use.
Mist the pillow corner 12 inches away, 30 minutes before sleep.
safetyNotes: Valerian is potent — start with half the dose if scent-sensitive.
Skip in pregnancy without OB approval.
Don't mist directly on skin — it's for fabric only.
recipes.storageTips: Bedside drawer. Light + heat ruin oils.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 3 drops bergamot for a brighter, This Works Deep Sleep–style top note.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Backed by research. Replaces the £25 This Works Pillow Spray.
recipes.socialHook: I'm sleeping 7 hrs again — and it's because of $5 of pillow spray.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: 1 a.m. doom-scroll. After: out by 10:45.

recipes.name: Focus Workspace Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Peppermint, rosemary, and lemon — a cognitive-clarity blend you can mist around the desk before deep work.
steps: Combine witch hazel + oils in the bottle. Swirl.
Top with distilled water.
Cap, shake gently, rest 30 min before use.
Mist 2–3 sprays around the desk before a focus block.
Re-mist every 90 minutes — your brain will know.
safetyNotes: Skip during pregnancy (rosemary).
Pet-toxic — never spray near cat lounging spots.
Avoid eyes.
recipes.storageTips: Cool drawer; the brighter the bottle, the faster oils fade.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 3 drops basil + 2 drops black pepper for the Aesop Istros studio scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Productivity-app energy in a bottle. Smells like a Notion office.
recipes.socialHook: Misted my desk and finally finished the deck.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: brain fog at 3 p.m. After: clear-headed for the next hour.

recipes.name: Bathroom Spa Steam Tabs
recipes.premiumDescription: Drop one on the shower floor and turn your morning into a 4-minute steam-room routine.
steps: Whisk baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch.
Add oils, mix evenly.
Spritz witch hazel a little at a time, mixing constantly until wet-sand consistency.
Press into mold firmly.
Cure 12 hours. Pop one on the shower floor (out of the spray) before turning on hot water.
safetyNotes: Eucalyptus is toxic to cats — keep them out of the bathroom during steam.
Avoid asthma triggers — start with a smaller dose.
Place out of the direct shower spray to maximize steam.
recipes.storageTips: Sealed jar, dry place. Moisture activates them prematurely.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 8 drops menthol crystals dissolved in oil for Bath & Body Works–style intensity.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Turns a regular shower into a Six Senses spa morning.
recipes.socialHook: I put a steam tab in my shower and turned my apartment into Aman.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: rushed morning shower. After: deep-breath spa moment.

recipes.name: Car Interior Luxe Cleaner
recipes.premiumDescription: Wipe-down spray for leather + vinyl + dashboards. Conditions while it cleans, leaves a soft cedar finish.
steps: Combine water, vinegar, and castile soap.
Add olive oil and oils — cap and shake hard for 20 seconds.
Spray on cloth, never on screens or fabric directly.
Wipe leather + vinyl in straight lines; buff with the second cloth.
Spray once on the floor mats and quick-vacuum after.
safetyNotes: Don't spray on infotainment screens or matte trim.
Test on a hidden seat seam first.
Shake before each spray — oil settles.
recipes.storageTips: Glove compartment OK in moderate climates.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Replace olive with jojoba + add 3 drops sandalwood for a CarPlay-era luxury cabin scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detail-shop quality at home. Smells like a new Range Rover.
recipes.socialHook: I detailed my car in 8 minutes and it smells like a $200K SUV.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: dust-line dashboard. After: showroom-fresh interior.

recipes.name: Closet Freshener Sachets
recipes.premiumDescription: Hand-tied muslin sachets that perfume your wardrobe and quietly repel moths. The Anthropologie shelf, but yours.
steps: Mix lavender buds, cedar shavings, and rose petals in a bowl.
Drop oils evenly across the dry mix; toss with a wooden spoon.
Let sit uncovered 1 hour to absorb.
Spoon into bags, pull drawstring closed, knot.
Tuck one in each drawer + closet shelf.
safetyNotes: Cedarwood EO can stain — don't pack with white silk.
Replace every 3 months for active scent + moth deterrence.
Keep out of reach of pets.
recipes.storageTips: Inside drawers — shake monthly to refresh.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Tie with linen ribbon + dried bay leaves for an heirloom aesthetic.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smells like grandma's good drawer + Loro Piana.
recipes.socialHook: Hand-tied closet sachets that look like Anthropologie and smell like Maison Margiela.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: stale wardrobe. After: opening drawers becomes a moment.

recipes.name: Jewelry Sparkle Dip
recipes.premiumDescription: Two-minute dip restores the shine on rings, earrings, and chains — without harsh ammonia.
steps: Line bowl with the foil shiny-side up.
Add baking soda + salt. Pour in warm water — fizzes briefly.
Stir in dish soap.
Submerge gold/silver pieces 2–3 minutes (skip pearls + opals + soft stones).
Lift, brush gently, rinse warm, dry with lint-free cloth.
safetyNotes: Skip pearls, opals, turquoise, emeralds — soft stones don't tolerate the dip.
Not for plated costume jewelry — can lift the plating.
Dispose of solution down the drain (not septic-safe in volume).
recipes.storageTips: Mix per session.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Finish with a 30-second buff using a soft jewelry rouge cloth.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Engagement-ring sparkle in 3 minutes for 30 cents.
recipes.socialHook: I dipped my ring in 30¢ of pantry stuff and it sparkles like the day he proposed.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: dulled gold. After: lit-from-within shine.

recipes.name: Wood Revival Polish
recipes.premiumDescription: Olive + lemon + beeswax polish that brings life back to dining tables, antiques, and butcher blocks.
steps: Melt beeswax + olive oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Remove from heat. Stir in lemon juice + oil — mixture will lighten.
Pour into the tin, let cool 1 hour to a soft balm.
Apply a thin layer with cloth, rub in circles.
Buff dry with a clean cloth for satin finish.
safetyNotes: Skip on raw or unfinished wood (will go blotchy).
Hot wax burns — handle the double boiler carefully.
Test in a hidden corner first.
recipes.storageTips: Cool, dark cabinet. Hardens fully when cold — warm in palms before use.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops cedarwood + 2 drops orange for an heirloom-furniture scent.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Makes thrift-store wood look intentional.
recipes.socialHook: Restored a $50 thrift table to $500 energy with this polish.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: dry, faded grain. After: deep, rich, hand-rubbed glow.

recipes.name: Scalp Reset Oil
recipes.premiumDescription: Pre-wash treatment that decongests buildup and balances oil. The reason your hair looks glassy on day one and great on day three.
steps: Combine jojoba and argan oils in the bottle.
Add tea tree, peppermint, lavender, vitamin E.
Cap, roll between palms 30 seconds.
Section dry hair, apply 6–8 drops directly to scalp.
Massage 2 minutes, rest 20–30 min, then double-shampoo out.
safetyNotes: Pet-toxic (tea tree, peppermint) — avoid pets sniffing while you treat.
Patch-test for sensitivity.
Skip during pregnancy with the peppermint.
recipes.storageTips: Amber bottle, dry cabinet.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 2 drops black pepper + 4 drops cedarwood for the Hair Rituel by Sisley dupe.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hair looks salon-clean for 3 days. Cures itchy buildup overnight.
recipes.socialHook: Pre-wash scalp oil that turned my hair into glass.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: itchy, flat second-day hair. After: glossy day three.

recipes.name: Dry Shampoo Powder
recipes.premiumDescription: Cornstarch + clay base that absorbs oil without the alcohol-spray crunch. Tinted versions for blondes and brunettes.
steps: Whisk arrowroot, cornstarch, kaolin clay in a bowl.
Brunettes: add cocoa to match shade — taste-test on a paper plate.
Drop in lavender oil, mix until distributed.
Transfer to shaker jar.
Dust onto roots with the brush. Wait 30 seconds. Massage. Tousle.
safetyNotes: Avoid the eye area — clay is irritating.
Less is more — over-applying = visible powder.
Skip if you have a corn allergy.
recipes.storageTips: Sealed jar, vanity drawer.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 1 tsp activated charcoal for an oil-magnet version for fine, oily hair.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Better than Batiste, no propellant cans, smells subtle.
recipes.socialHook: I made dry shampoo that doesn't smell like a teen rave.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: greasy roots by hour 12. After: matte and full all day three.

recipes.name: Mosquito Bite Relief Balm
recipes.premiumDescription: Solid balm in a tin — cools, calms, stops the itch in 30 seconds. Travel-essentials shelf.
steps: Melt coconut oil + beeswax + shea butter in a double boiler.
Remove from heat. Cool 1 minute (oils degrade in heat).
Stir in essential oils.
Pour into tin. Cool 1 hour to harden.
Dab a fingertip on bite — itch fades in seconds.
safetyNotes: Not for under age 6 (peppermint).
Avoid open wounds and broken skin.
Keep away from cats.
recipes.storageTips: Cool drawer or travel pouch. Soft above 80°F.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops blue tansy for a deep-blue balm with extra anti-inflammatory hit.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: One tin replaces five After Bite tubes. Dad-bag essential.
recipes.socialHook: The tin in my carry-on that makes mosquito bites disappear.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: 3 days of red welts. After: 30 seconds and gone.

recipes.name: Allergy Season Air Mist
recipes.premiumDescription: Spray on pillows, drapes, and the entryway — settles airborne pollen and clears the breath at the door.
steps: Combine witch hazel and oils in the bottle. Swirl.
Top with distilled water; cap and rest 30 minutes.
Mist drapes, doormats, and pillow corners during high-pollen days.
Pair with HEPA filter use for best results.
Refresh entryway daily during peak season.
safetyNotes: Pet-toxic blend — never spray near pet beds or cat lounging spots.
Avoid eyes.
Don't replace asthma medication — supplement only.
recipes.storageTips: Entryway shelf — bottle visibility = habitual use.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 4 drops Roman chamomile + 4 drops blue tansy for an allergy-soothing version.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Allergy-season survival in a bottle. Smells like a eucalyptus spa.
recipes.socialHook: The spring mist that's saving my sinuses (and my couch).
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: pollen-dust on every surface. After: settled air, easier breathing.

recipes.name: Signature Home Welcome Spray
recipes.premiumDescription: Two sprays at the entry hall before guests arrive — your home's signature scent. Layered fig, cedar, and white florals.
steps: Pour vodka in the bottle.
Add fig, cedar, neroli, white tea oils.
Cap, gently swirl 30 seconds.
Top with distilled water; rest 72 hours for the scent to bloom.
Two sprays at the entryway 5 minutes before guests.
safetyNotes: Cat-toxic (cedar, neroli) — avoid spraying low on furniture cats use.
Spot-test on rugs — can stain light fibers.
Flammable while wet.
recipes.storageTips: Foyer console. Amber bottle protects from fading.
recipes.premiumUpgrade: Add 3 drops black currant + 2 drops cassis for a Diptyque Philosykos-inspired entry.
recipes.whyPeopleLoveIt: Guests ask: 'what does your house smell like?' — and remember it.
recipes.socialHook: I gave my home a signature scent and my guests have not stopped texting.
recipes.beforeAfter: Before: nothing. After: a moment people walk in and notice.
